# Figure description: Картограмма_бурения.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/plan_data/Картограмма_бурения.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index map** (схема / scheme) showing the spatial distribution of drilling works (буровых работ) conducted by third-party / external organizations (сторонних организаций), whose data/materials were incorporated into the report. It is a reference compilation map showing the areal coverage of prior investigations used as source data for Report 36572.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a multi-sheet area spanning both **N-42 and M-42 series** map sheets at 1:200,000 scale. The grid cells visible correspond to standard Soviet topographic nomenclature sheets, including:

- **XXXII, XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV** (upper/northern row)
- **II, III, IV, V** (middle row)
- **VII, VIII, IX, X, XI** (middle-lower row)
- **XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII** (southern row)

The boundary between **N-series (north)** and **M-series (south)** sheets is explicitly marked with the notation **"N / M"** on the right margin. This places the survey area straddling the N-42 and M-42 map sheet zones in northern Kazakhstan.

---

## Key Content

The figure uses **differently hatched and patterned polygons** (at least 8–10 distinct fill patterns) to distinguish the areal coverage of individual prior drilling campaigns by different authors/organizations. Each polygon is labeled with an **author surname and year**, indicating the geographic footprint of each organization's borehole data.

Key spatial observations:
- The **densest concentration of prior work** is in the central portion of the map, particularly around sheets **III, IX, and XIV–XV**, where multiple overlapping polygons from different campaigns are present.
- The **northern sheets (XXXII–XXXV)** have sparser coverage, with only a few named campaigns.
- The **eastern sheets (X, XI, XVI, XVII)** appear largely **blank**, indicating little or no prior third-party drilling data was available from those areas.
- **Western sheets (VII, VIII, XIII)** show moderate coverage.

**Distinct fill patterns** used (differentiating organizations/reports) include:
- Diagonal hatching (multiple orientations: NE, NW, cross-hatch)
- Dotted/stipple patterns (fine and coarse dots)
- Wavy horizontal lines
- Diamond/rhombus cross-hatch
- Triangle/dot combinations
- Vertical lines
- Blank/unfilled areas (no prior data)

No formal legend box is visible, but each polygon is self-labeled with the author name and year, serving as an embedded legend.

## Research Value

This figure serves as a **data provenance and spatial coverage map**, allowing a geologist or research assistant to immediately assess **which sub-areas have existing borehole data** from prior campaigns and
